Tool Call: retrieve("role of alkaline phosphatase and pyrophosphatase in calcification")

After reviewing the retrieved evidence, I find strong support for option C — alkaline phosphatase and pyrophosphatase — as critical players in calcification regulation. Alkaline phosphatase facilitates mineralization by generating inorganic phosphate within matrix vesicles during bone formation, whereas pyrophosphatase prevents pathological calcification by breaking down pyrophosphate, which otherwise acts as a potent inhibitor of crystal growth. These dual regulatory mechanisms reflect a precise balance necessary for proper skeletal development and prevention of ectopic calcification.
